summary:
• Ukraine's sustained drone attacks on Russian refining infrastructure create ongoing disruption risk to regional supply chains, with recent strikes on major Gazprom facilities threatening European energy security.

• Iran's geopolitical posturing around the Strait of Hormuz combined with uncertain U.S. negotiations introduces volatility to global crude flows, particularly affecting Western Hemisphere refiners dependent on Middle Eastern supply.

• Colombia's natural gas crisis is being compounded by external supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz closure, signaling how interconnected global energy markets amplify production shortfalls in vulnerable downstream economies.
